votes.reason: The Python code implements the exact same Caesar cipher logic (shift by 4 on lowercase letters) as the Coq code, using equivalent string and character operations.
The Python code correctly implements the Caesar cipher logic (shift by 4) for lowercase characters, exactly matching the Coq specification.
The Python code implements the same character rotation logic (Caesar cipher shift +4 for lowercase) and string mapping as the Coq definitions.

The Python code correctly implements the same logic as the Coq code: is_lowercase checks if ASCII value is between 97-122, rotate_char shifts lowercase letters by 4 positions with wraparound mod 26, and encrypt_model applies rotate_char to each character. The encrypt_spec checks equality with the model result. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

The encrypt_spec checks equality with the model result. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

Both shift lowercase ASCII letters by 4 (wrapping within a–z) and leave other characters unchanged; encrypt_spec checks result equals encrypt_model.
Both shift lowercase ASCII letters by 4 modulo 26 and leave all other characters unchanged; encrypt_model and encrypt_spec match this behavior.
Python mirrors the Coq logic: it shifts lowercase ASCII letters by 4 with wraparound, leaves other chars unchanged, and encrypt_spec checks equality. Behaviors match on all ASCII inputs.
